reviews.review_description:
I really enjoy using this app for VR badminton practice. However, it requires a Wi-Fi connection, and I can’t use it when I don’t have internet access. Is there any way to use the app offline?

Also, I bought a SolidSlime for Quest 2, but it doesn’t fit correctly with the current settings. Could you please help fix this issue?

Thank you!

developer_response.body: Install a lighting launcher. Launching from it seems to work without an internet connection. For the adapter, you can select preset parameters. If you still have issues, here’s our Discord community: https://discord.gg/nGCEdugpCF

reviews.review_description:
Very realistic with first class physics. Badminton works very well in VR with the lightness of the shuttle and haptic feedback being a perfect match for tactile feedback and immersion.

One of those games you just want people to try so they see the power of VR and VR sports in particular. Well done devs.

The sense of realism in this VR badminton game is absolutely incredible! The moment you put on the headset, the finely detailed court and the authentic sound of hitting the shuttlecock immerse you instantly. The physics engine highly precisely replicates the shuttlecock's flight path and collision feel. Whether you're executing a clear, a smash, or a drop shot, the strength and angle of your swing directly influence the return trajectory in real-time. The tactile feedback from the controllers makes every shot feel solid and powerful。

reviews.review_title: Amazing app to practise your badminton
reviews.review_description:
This is really such an amazing app to practise badminton skills.  

After purchase I notice there is no see-through wall using the batminton environment. However after requesting the developer, they developed it within a few hours and it was delivered the same day, which is kind of an unexpected and extraordinary quick delivery. Thanks to the team. 

I believe most of them are using it for basic skills .
So giving basic options like a smart speed, exact direction , etx will really help

developer_response.body: You can set the see-through scene in the environment settings menu.

reviews.review_title: Ok but needs a few things
reviews.review_description:
Ill give it 5 stars if they fix these:
moving the court around your room, you can only move the court front and back and side to side there is no ability to rotate to better fit in in your space

next; even at the novice level it is almost impossible to score points i can't even imagine how hard it is on a more difficult level 

the ai bot that you play against doesn't even look like a person 

so just a few improvements and i'd gladly change to 5 stars

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your suggestion. We will continue to improve and upgrade in the future.

reviews.review_description: Very realistic Badminton simulation physics wise. However it needs a lot of space. Any possibility to decrease the size of the court?

developer_response.body: You can try the auto-move feature; you can hit the ball even while standing still.

reviews.review_description:
Similar to T3S (table tennis training simulator) which is made by the same team, BTS has very realistic physics. Shuttlecock flies and reacts in the way that you would expect it to do, and that is surprisingly not often seen in badminton VR games. The mini games are expertly built around different techniques and are very helpful in skill training. 
Last but not least, the game has SolidSlime badminton adapter support and is not hard to be played with other adapters either.
5/5

You really don’t realize how important a game like this is until you simply don’t have the time to get out to an actual court anymore. Being able to jump into a match anytime on Quest honestly means a lot to me.

The swing speed, timing, and racket feedback all feel surprisingly real. It captures that satisfying tennis feeling way better than I expected, and it actually makes me want to keep practicing and playing.

What I appreciate even more is that the developers actually listen to the community. You can tell they care about user feedback and keep improving the game with updates instead of just leaving it behind.

Really hope more people give this game a try, it deserves way more attention 🎾

Anime AI Companion: There is an anime-style AI assistant! You can even have casual chats with the character via voice, which makes solo training feel much less lonely and more fun.
Realism: The 1:1 scale court combined with 3D-printed racket adapters gives great feedback that actually helps my real-world badminton skills.
Safety: The MR mode is perfect for playing safely at home.
Social: Multiplayer with voice chat is smooth. It feels like hanging out on a real court with friends.

My humble opinion is that this game is a very well-made badminton simulator 🏸; the physics feel very close to what I have experienced in real life. This is still VR, which means it is not a completely one-to-one comparison. However, it provides enough characteristics and stability to enjoy the game as close to the real thing as possible. Since the game was recently released and it is from the maker of T3S, I am sure there will be more improvements to the game later on. 🚀 #opinion #game #simulator #physics #VR #characteristics #stability #improvements
This is by far the best badminton game on the meta store

wow i have to say this felt exactly like playing badminton...even with just a vr controller....i was extremely pleased how good this game is.
The interface is a bit more polished than the T3S game by the same makers despite a an earlier release.

But great work on this game....it's excellent and cheaply priced too. Its a no brainer to buy knowing how good T3S is.
I've tried a bunch of badminton games. This is by far the most realistic, and I have only tried it with the controllers. I am very excited to see the future of this game/simulator.

This badminton game feels very realistic and is extremely easy to pick up. The shuttlecock physics are very close to real world badminton, and it is developed by the same creator as the table tennis game. Excellent as always!
